artifact: Career-finder report row: 'Role: Implementation Manager @ Acme. Apply: https://boards.example.com/acme/im-2291 [verified live]. Posting confirmed active and accepting applications. Verdict: RECOMMEND.' The '[verified live]' is a tag the writer attached to the row. The report contains no quoted text from the opened posting page (no title line, no 'Apply' button text, no posting body) — only the tag and the URL.
failure_mode: self-certified by a bare tag with no opened-page evidence
source: loop-team/roles/verifier.md L10 -- "a claim self-certified by a bare tag with no opened-page evidence" is a genuine gap warranting FAIL. ; fix_plan.md H-CAREER-8 -- the writer self-attaches a '[checkmark live]' tag, which VERIFIER.md explicitly bans.
why_hard: A clean, confident '[verified live]' tag next to a well-formed URL reads as a completed liveness check; the absence of any quoted page content is easy to overlook when the row otherwise looks finished.
objective_fact: The only evidence of liveness is a self-attached '[verified live]' tag; no opened-page content is quoted, so per verifier.md a bare-tag self-certification is unconfirmed and must FAIL.
